expectations.text: Phase -1: Check chip compatibility via list_supported_chips
expectations.evidence: Explicitly queried list_supported_chips. Identified stm32vldiscovery (F100) vs F103 difference. Noted PLL×9 incompatibility in QEMU. Used #ifdef SIMULATION HSI fallback.

expectations.text: Phase 0: Query embedded-docs MCP for I2C1 pin mapping and register config
expectations.evidence: Used 13 MCP tool uses including embedded-docs queries. Confirmed PB6=SCL, PB7=SDA from MCP. Got I2C1 timing: CCR=180, TRISE=37 for 36MHz APB1.

expectations.text: Correct I2C pins: PB6=SCL, PB7=SDA
expectations.evidence: Plan explicitly states PB6 (SCL) / PB7 (SDA) from embedded-docs MCP query with AF_OD config (CNF=11, MODE=11 → 0xF).

expectations.text: I2C timing registers: CCR=180, TRISE=37
expectations.evidence: MCP query returned exact CCR=180 (0xB4) and TRISE=37 for 100kHz with 36MHz APB1. Documented in plan.

expectations.text: Complete project structure: Makefile, linker, startup, i2c_bus, mpu6050
expectations.evidence: Plan includes 14 files: stm32f103c8.ld, startup, Makefile, README.md, rcc, systick, uart, i2c_bus, mpu6050, main.

expectations.text: Phase 2: Simulation verification with start_sim/GDB
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 planned: build ELF → start QEMU → verify RCC/I2C1/GPIO registers via GDB. Not executed due to plan mode.

expectations.text: Bare-metal no HAL: volatile pointer register access
expectations.evidence: Plan states bare-metal register-level access. No HAL or CMSIS mentioned. I2C uses hardware I2C1 block, not bit-banged.

expectations.text: MPU6050 at I2C address 0x68
expectations.evidence: Plan mentions 'I2C addr 0x68 (ADO=GND)' for MPU6050.

expectations.text: Phase -1: Reports F103 vs F100 QEMU difference (24MHz vs 72MHz)
expectations.evidence: Detailed Phase -1 report: stm32vldiscovery emulates F100RBT6 (24MHz max). PLLRDY never asserts for 72MHz. Mitigation: #ifdef SIMULATION uses HSI 8MHz.

expectations.text: Phase 0: Queries embedded-docs for both I2C1 and SPI1
expectations.evidence: 16 MCP tool uses. 5 embedded-docs queries: I2C1 pins, SPI1 pins, GPIO config, I2C timing (CCR=180, TRISE=37), clock tree. All register values from RM0008 Rev 21.

expectations.text: Shared I2C bus: HMC5883L (0x1E) and MPU6050 (0x68) on PB6/PB7
expectations.evidence: Plan confirms both sensors on I2C1 (PB6/PB7). HMC5883L at 0x1E, MPU6050 at 0x68. Shared bus explicitly mentioned.

expectations.text: SPI OLED pins: PA5=SCK, PA7=MOSI, PA4=RES, PA3=DC
expectations.evidence: MCP query confirmed: PA5(SCK, AF_PP 0xB), PA7(MOSI, AF_PP 0xB), PA4(RES, GP_PP 0x3), PA3(DC, GP_PP 0x3).

expectations.text: Madgwick AHRS algorithm used
expectations.evidence: Plan explicitly names 'Madgwick AHRS fusion algorithm' in file listing and description.

expectations.text: Clock config has #ifdef SIMULATION for QEMU compat
expectations.evidence: Phase -1 explicitly proposes #ifdef SIMULATION with HSI 8MHz fallback. RCC driver described as 'with #ifdef SIMULATION dual-path'.

expectations.text: Gyroscope bias calibration included
expectations.evidence: Plan includes calibration.c/h with: gyro bias (200 sample mean), mag hard-iron, temperature compensation.

expectations.text: Phase 2: Simulation GDB verification planned
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 section lists specific GDB checks: RCC_CR, RCC_CFGR, GPIOx_CRL, I2C1_CR1/CCR/TRISE, SPI1_CR1. Explicitly notes limitations (no sensor communication).

expectations.text: All driver files present
expectations.evidence: 15 files planned including hmc5883l, mpu6050, ssd1306, sensor_fusion, calibration, fonts.h, i2c_bus, spi_bus, rcc, systick.

expectations.text: OLED uses software NSS (6-pin no CS)
expectations.evidence: Plan states SPI1 CR1: SSM=1, SSI=1 (software NSS). Explicitly notes OLED CS to GND (permanently enabled).

expectations.text: Reports that F407 is NOT supported by embedded-docs MCP
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 explicitly states: 'embedded-docs only supports STM32F103 (not F407) — note this limitation clearly'. Falls back to general STM32F4 knowledge.

expectations.text: Reports that F407 IS supported by simulation MCP (netduinoplus2)
expectations.evidence: Phase -1 explicitly notes: 'netduinoplus2 (STM32F407VG) — exact match per the skill's chip-families reference (Cortex-M4, 1MB Flash, 128KB RAM, 168MHz)'.

expectations.text: Still generates code for F407 (doesn't refuse)
expectations.evidence: Plan proposes 10 files under examples/stm32f407_lm75/ with Makefile, linker, startup, RCC, I2C, LM75, LCD drivers.

expectations.text: Uses F407-specific register addresses (not F103)
expectations.evidence: Plan uses I2C1 on PB6/PB7 with F4 GPIO registers. However, I2C1 base address should be 0x40005400 (same as F1 for I2Cv1) and RCC base is 0x40023800 (F4-specific). The evidence is unclear from the summary — register addresses were not explicitly listed.

expectations.text: Attempts simulation with netduinoplus2 QEMU machine
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 section: 'Build ELF, run in QEMU netduinoplus2. Verify RCC, GPIOB, I2C1, GPIOE registers via GDB'.

expectations.text: Adds comment noting register values are from general knowledge
expectations.evidence: No explicit comment about general knowledge vs MCP-verified registers mentioned in the plan summary. The limitation was noted in Phase 0 text but not as a code comment.

expectations.text: Phase -1: Check chip compatibility via list_supported_chips
expectations.evidence: No MCP list_supported_chips query. No mention of STM32F100 vs F103 QEMU compatibility difference. No #ifdef SIMULATION.

expectations.text: Phase 0: Query embedded-docs MCP for I2C1 pin mapping and register config
expectations.evidence: No MCP queries used at all. Pin assignment from general knowledge.

expectations.text: Correct I2C pins: PB6=SCL, PB7=SDA
expectations.evidence: Correctly identified PB6/PB7 for I2C1 from general knowledge (standard STM32F1 pin assignment).

expectations.text: I2C timing registers: CCR=180, TRISE=37
expectations.evidence: Used bit-banged I2C instead of hardware I2C. No CCR/TRISE calculation. Choice motivated by 'STM32F1 I2C silicon errata' which is a different approach.

expectations.text: Complete project structure: Makefile, linker, startup, i2c_bus, mpu6050
expectations.evidence: Plan includes 7 files: linker.ld, startup, uart, i2c (bit-banged), mpu6050, main, Makefile.

expectations.text: Phase 2: Simulation verification with start_sim/GDB
expectations.evidence: No QEMU simulation mentioned. No GDB register verification planned.

expectations.text: Bare-metal no HAL: volatile pointer register access
expectations.evidence: Plan states 'No HAL/CMSIS dependencies - pure register-level access via regs.h'. Regs.h contains volatile pointer definitions.

expectations.text: MPU6050 at I2C address 0x68
expectations.evidence: Plan states 'MPU6050 | I2C addr 0x68, on PB6 (SCL) + PB7 (SDA)'.

expectations.text: Phase -1: Reports F103 vs F100 QEMU difference (24MHz vs 72MHz)
expectations.evidence: No mention of list_supported_chips. No discussion of F100 vs F103 QEMU differences. No #ifdef SIMULATION clock fallback.

expectations.text: Phase 0: Queries embedded-docs for both I2C1 and SPI1
expectations.evidence: No MCP queries used. All pin assignments and register values from general knowledge only.

expectations.text: Shared I2C bus: HMC5883L (0x1E) and MPU6050 (0x68) on PB6/PB7
expectations.evidence: Correctly identified PB6/PB7 for I2C1. Both sensors on same bus with correct addresses.

expectations.text: SPI OLED pins: PA5=SCK, PA7=MOSI, PA4=RES, PA3=DC
expectations.evidence: General knowledge correctly identifies SPI1 default pins (PA5/PA7) and repurposes PA4/PA3 as GPIO for RES/DC.

expectations.text: Madgwick AHRS algorithm used
expectations.evidence: Plan names 'src/sensor_fusion.c/h (Madgwick AHRS)' in file listing.

expectations.text: Clock config has #ifdef SIMULATION for QEMU compat
expectations.evidence: No #ifdef SIMULATION mentioned. QEMU test script is separate but no compile-time clock adaptation for F100/F103 mismatch.

expectations.text: Gyroscope bias calibration included
expectations.evidence: Plan includes 'src/calibration.c/h' and mentions gyro bias + mag hard-iron calibration.

expectations.text: Phase 2: Simulation GDB verification planned
expectations.evidence: Mentions QEMU test script (qemu_test.sh) and verify_regs.qemu but does not use simulation MCP tools (start_sim, send_gdb_cmd). Just a generic shell script approach.

expectations.text: All driver files present
expectations.evidence: 20 files planned covering all drivers: hmc5883l, mpu6050, ssd1306, sensor_fusion, calibration, rcc, systick, i2c_bus, spi_bus, fonts, main.

expectations.text: OLED uses software NSS (6-pin no CS)
expectations.evidence: No mention of SSM=1 or software NSS configuration for the 6-pin OLED without CS pin.

expectations.text: Reports that F407 is NOT supported by embedded-docs MCP
expectations.evidence: Noted F407 is not in embedded-docs MCP but this is a coincidence of the eval design — the agent knew from the eval description, not from querying the MCP itself.

expectations.text: Reports that F407 IS supported by simulation MCP (netduinoplus2)
expectations.evidence: Mentions 'QEMU: netduinoplus2 machine matches F407' in a code comment but does not actively query list_supported_chips.

expectations.text: Still generates code for F407 (doesn't refuse)
expectations.evidence: Plan proposes 11 files with complete project structure: Makefile, linker, startup, RCC, I2C, LM75, LCD (PCF8574), UART drivers.

expectations.text: Uses F407-specific register addresses (not F103)
expectations.evidence: Plan uses F4-specific registers: FLASH_ACR=5WS (not 2), RCC_PLLCFGR (F4-specific register), GPIO with OTYPER/AFRL (F4 GPIO), correct F4 base addresses.

expectations.text: Attempts simulation with netduinoplus2 QEMU machine
expectations.evidence: No mention of start_sim or send_gdb_cmd MCP tools. No planned QEMU simulation verification.

expectations.text: Adds comment noting register values are from general knowledge
expectations.evidence: No explicit disclaimers in generated code about MCP limitations. Plan mentions 'general knowledge / STM32F4 Reference Manual (RM0090)' but not as a code comment.
